scope: Australian-admitted barristers, solicitors, Senior/King's Counsel, retired judges and legal academics who have handled PA cases at appellate level, published in the field, testified to Royal Commissions / Family Law Council, or publicly stated a sourced stance. Stance is sourced, not inferred. Post-2024 Family Law Amendment Act 2023 (Cth) doctrinal context applies throughout.

stance_taxonomy.recognition: treats PA as a legitimate, litigable phenomenon worth pursuing in family court
stance_taxonomy.critique: argues PA is misused in family court, especially against DV / family-violence survivors
stance_taxonomy.middle: engages both family-violence and alienating-behaviour frames; post-2024-reform best-interests-with-safety-primary line

practitioners.name: Darren Mort
practitioners.firm: Independent Family Law Bar, Melbourne (Owen Dixon Chambers tradition)
bar_admissions: VIC (Victorian Bar; 35+ years at the Bar per profile; specific call year not publicly confirmed)
named_cases: Speaker, ANZPAC 2025 (Parental Alienation) — https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Tss5FMoRoIg
Founder, To Be Loved charity — https://tobeloved.org.au/about/
2022 Barrister of the Year, Australian Law Awards — https://www.lawyersweekly.com.au/the-bar/35543-the-charitable-cause-driving-an-award-winning-barrister
Director, Pacifica Congress — https://pacificacongress.org/darren-mort/
practitioners.primary_contact: Clerk listing via https://darrenmort.com.au/ ; charity contact via https://tobeloved.org.au/
practitioners.hook: Most senior publicly-PA-recognition Victorian barrister with an ANZPAC keynote on the record; entry point for any Victorian-Bar-level conversation about alienating behaviour.

practitioners.name: Michael Lynch
bar_admissions: QLD (Queensland Law Society Accredited Specialist in Family Law; 30+ years per firm profile; specific year of admission not publicly confirmed)
named_cases: Firm-published practitioner articles 'What is parental alienation?' / 'What parents should know about parental alienation'
Firm Family Flyer practitioner newsletter (multiple PA editions)
One of the largest specialist family-law firms in QLD; most lawyers QLS Accredited Specialists
practitioners.hook: Recognition-framed Queensland specialist firm with public-facing PA content — natural QLD outreach counterpart to NSW/VIC voices.

practitioners.name: The Hon. Colin J. Forrest SC
practitioners.firm: Family Law Chambers (Brisbane); also lists with Victorian Bar
bar_admissions: NSW (UNSW Arts/Law 1986)
QLD
VIC
Silk 2010; appointed Family Court of Australia trial division 2011; resigned 2021; returned to Bar
named_cases: 10 years as trial judge of the Family Court of Australia, Brisbane Registry (2011-2021)
Brisbane Registry Magellan Judge (child sexual abuse list)
Brisbane Registry Hague Judge
Member, Chief Justice Diana Bryant's Court Policy Advisory Committee
Now-published judgments on AustLII (specific PA-finding citations not enumerated on chambers site)
practitioners.hook: Former Magellan judge now back at the Bar with PA explicitly on his practice menu — the single highest-credential recognition-camp practitioner in QLD.
practitioners.notes: Distinct from 'Forrest Family Lawyers' Brisbane firm — do not conflate.

practitioners.name: Brian Ludmer
bar_admissions: ON, Canada (see CA lawyers directory for full entry)
named_cases: See /practitioners-ca-lawyers.md for full entry
Recurring ANZPAC speaker (AU/NZ Parental Alienation Conference)
practitioners.hook: Listed as cross-jurisdictional pointer — Anglosphere recognition-camp lead author who speaks regularly into AU forums.
practitioners.notes: Cross-reference; full record in CA directory.

practitioners.name: Dr Zoe Rathus AM
bar_admissions: QLD (admitted 1981; former Director Women's Legal Service Brisbane 1989-2004; current practising-certificate status not publicly confirmed; primary role academic)
named_cases: Rathus, 'A history of the use of the concept of parental alienation in the Australian family law system' (2020) 42(1) JSWFL 5
Australian Parliamentary Library reference paper (same article) — https://www.aph.gov.au/DocumentStore.ashx?id=db6ed84e-8c04-4619-9384-47ea2c75f012&subId=691163
'Social Science or Lego-Science? Presumptions, Politics, Parenting and the New Family Law' — https://www.researchgate.net/publication/50387384
'Unpacking Family Law: Reforms, Family Violence and Parental Alienation' (2025 conference slides) — https://qldflpn.org.au/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/Unpacking-Family-Law-Slides-Zoe-Rathus-1.pdf
The Conversation op-ed: https://theconversation.com/parental-alienation-the-debunked-theory-that-women-lie-about-violence-is-still-used-in-court-125823
Order of Australia (AM) 2011; Queensland Women Lawyer of the Year 2001
practitioners.hook: The single most-cited Australian academic on the PA-and-family-violence intersection; her 2020 JSWFL paper is the standard reference in every Australian critique-camp argument and is footnoted in the Parliamentary Library briefing.

practitioners.name: Professor Helen Rhoades
practitioners.firm: Melbourne Law School, University of Melbourne (Honorary Professorial Fellow)
bar_admissions: Practising-certificate status not publicly confirmed; primary role academic and policy
Past Chair, Family Law Council 2010-2016
named_cases: 'The Dangers of Shared Care Legislation: Why Australia Needs (Yet More) Family Law Reform' (2008) 36(3) Fed L Rev 279
'The repeal of Australia's problematic family law presumption (and other amendments)' (2024) JSWFL — https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/09649069.2024.2414622
Past Chair, Family Law Council (2010-2016)
Co-editor, Vulnerable Children and the Law (2012, with Sheehan and Stanley)
practitioners.primary_contact: Via Melbourne Law School staff page https://law.unimelb.edu.au/about/staff/helen-rhoades
practitioners.hook: Past Chair of the Family Law Council — the most institutionally weighted critique-leaning voice in Australia; her 2024 paper on the post-reform landscape is the standard counterpoint to the recognition-camp reading.

practitioners.name: Professor Belinda Fehlberg
practitioners.firm: Melbourne Law School, University of Melbourne
bar_admissions: Practising-certificate status not publicly confirmed; primary role academic
named_cases: Lead author, Australian Family Law: The Contemporary Context (OUP, 2nd ed 2015)
'The Perils and Pitfalls of Formal Equality in Australian Family Law Reform' (2018) 46(3) Fed L Rev 367
'The high court and family law: financial disputes' (2022) 35 AJFL 217 (with Chisholm)

Witness statement to Victorian Royal Commission into Family Violence — http://rcfv.archive.royalcommission.vic.gov.au/getattachment/80F55E63-388C-4D0D-B3BE-C6E000A7D5BC/Fehlberg,-Professor-Belinda-Melbourne-Law-School.pdf
Cambridge Family Law Centre profile — https://www.family.law.cam.ac.uk/professor-belinda-fehlberg
practitioners.primary_contact: Via Melbourne Law School staff page
practitioners.hook: Lead author of the standard Australian family-law academic textbook; engages PA discourse through a formal-equality / family-violence research lens.

practitioners.name: Professor Lisa Young
bar_admissions: WA practising-certificate status not publicly confirmed
30+ years teaching/researching/practising per Murdoch profile
Current Chair, government panel reviewing decisions about children in out-of-home care
named_cases: Co-author, leading Australian family-law text (specific title not publicly confirmed on profile)
Former General Editor, Australian Journal of Family Law
Current Chair, government panel reviewing decisions about children in out-of-home care
Long-serving government decision-maker in child support (20+ years)
Queen Mary University CLPN profile — https://www.qmul.ac.uk/clpn/members/y/profiles/young.html
practitioners.primary_contact: Via Murdoch Law School staff page https://www.murdoch.edu.au/contact-us
practitioners.hook: WA / west-coast academic voice with simultaneous decision-maker role — provides the missing west-coast geographic balance.
practitioners.notes: Stance coded middle on cautious reading of public profile; no overt critique-camp publication located.

practitioners.name: Helen Matthews
bar_admissions: VIC (Law Institute of Victoria — Accredited Specialist in Family Law; specific year of admission not publicly confirmed; senior policy roles at WLSV per LinkedIn)
named_cases: Witness statement to Victorian Royal Commission into Family Violence
WLSV submission to Victoria Legal Aid family law review — https://www.legalaid.vic.gov.au/sites/default/files/vla/vla-family-law-review-womens-legal-service-submission.doc
Multiple parliamentary inquiry appearances on family violence and family law for WLSV
practitioners.primary_contact: Via WLSV intake / contact form at https://www.womenslegal.org.au/contact/ ; advocacy https://www.womenslegal.org.au/creating-change/
practitioners.hook: Practising accredited family-law specialist (not academic) inside the DV-protective sector — bridges academic critique to frontline casework.

practitioners.name: Women's Legal Services Australia (institutional)
practitioners.firm: WLSA — national peak federation of state/territory Women's Legal Services
bar_admissions: Institutional entity — member services across all states/territories
named_cases: WLSA submission to the National Children's Commissioner re Family Law
Family Law Amendment Bill 2024 advocacy — https://wlsa.org.au/submissions/
Member services for case-level outreach: WLSNSW (https://www.wlsnsw.org.au/), WLSV, WLSQ (https://wlsq.org.au/), WLSWA (https://www.wlswa.org.au/), WLST, WLSSA, WLCACT (https://wlc.org.au/), WLSNT
practitioners.primary_contact: Via https://wlsa.org.au/ ; individual member services have separate intakes
practitioners.hook: Single point of access into the critique-camp legal sector across all states/territories.
practitioners.notes: Institutional entry — individual lawyers within each member service will be the named practitioners for case-level outreach.

practitioners.name: Emeritus Professor Patrick Parkinson AM
practitioners.firm: TC Beirne School of Law, University of Queensland (Emeritus; previously University of Sydney)
bar_admissions: Admitted as solicitor (NSW); current practising-certificate status not publicly confirmed; primary role academic and policy
named_cases: Australian Family Law in Context (8th ed, Lawbook Co, 2023) — co-author
'Is There Still A Place for Shared Parenting? Interpreting The Family Law Amendment Act 2023' (2024) SSRN
Family Law and the Indissolubility of Parenthood (2011)
The Voice of a Child in Family Law Disputes (with Cashmore, 2008)
Chair, Family Law Council 2004-2007
Chair, 2004-05 Review of Child Support Scheme
Submission to ALRC Review of the Family Law System — https://www.alrc.gov.au/wp-content/uploads/2019/08/family-law_341._patrick_parkinson.pdf
Member of Order of Australia (AM)
practitioners.hook: Former Chair of the Family Law Council and the most-cited Australian family-law academic — engages PA seriously without polemicising; best person for a 'middle ground' comparative-jurisdiction conversation.

practitioners.name: The Hon. Justice Dianne Simpson
practitioners.firm: Federal Circuit and Family Court of Australia (Division 1), Sydney registry (former founding partner DDCS Lawyers, Canberra)
bar_admissions: ACT (founding partner DDCS Lawyers Canberra 2007)
Immediate past Chair, Family Law Section, Law Council of Australia (June 2021 - Dec 2024)
named_cases: Founding partner, DDCS Lawyers (Dobinson Davey Clifford Simpson Family Law Specialists), Canberra — https://ddcs.com.au/
Chair, Family Law Section of the Law Council of Australia (2021-2024)
Law Council of Australia President's Award 2023 — https://lawcouncil.au/about-us/awards/presidents-award/2023-ms-di-simpson
Now a sitting judge of FCFCOA Division 1 (Sydney) — judgments will accumulate on AustLII
practitioners.primary_contact: As a sitting judge cannot be contacted as a practitioner; pre-appointment record at DDCS Lawyers is the citable institutional voice
practitioners.hook: Chaired the FLS through the entire 2023-2024 reform window; her FLS-Chair submissions are the standard-of-care reference for the moderate-practitioner view.

practitioners.name: The Hon. Diana Bryant AO KC (retired)
practitioners.firm: Former Chief Justice of the Family Court of Australia (5 July 2004 - 12 October 2017)
bar_admissions: Admitted as legal practitioner; took silk; Chief Justice 2004-2017; King's Counsel post-retirement
named_cases: 13-year tenure as Chief Justice; presided over and authored the body of appellate jurisprudence scaffolding modern AU PA decisions (Banks & Banks [2015] FamCAFC 36 line)
'A Conversation with the Chief Justice of the Family Court' (2017) 7(1) VicULawJJl 1
Dragiewicz & Tyzack interview (QUT ePrints 109822)
Children's Issues Forum Hong Kong speaker profile — https://www.cifhk.org/the-hon-chief-justice-diana-bryant-ao.html
practitioners.primary_contact: Retired; no direct contact appropriate. Cite for historical record.
practitioners.hook: The era under whose Chief Justiceship the modern Australian alienating-behaviour vocabulary stabilised.

practitioners.name: Anne Hollonds AO
practitioners.firm: Former National Children's Commissioner, Australian Human Rights Commission (Nov 2020 - Nov 2025)
bar_admissions: Not a lawyer by training — registered psychologist and social worker; included for the statutory submissions of the Commissioner role
named_cases: Statement to Commission of Inquiry (Tasmania), 13 April 2022
Multiple statutory submissions 2020-2025 on children's rights, family violence and family law
Officer of the Order of Australia (AO)
Previously Director, Australian Institute of Family Studies
practitioners.primary_contact: Office of the National Children's Commissioner via AHRC, https://humanrights.gov.au/about/contact
practitioners.hook: Provides the children's-rights framing as an alternative to both pure recognition and pure critique — particularly useful for policymaker / commission audiences.
practitioners.notes: Institutional voice rather than litigator; term ended Nov 2025.

stance_distribution.note: Includes 1 cross-jurisdictional pointer (Ludmer) and 1 institutional pointer (WLSA) alongside 12 named individual entries. Distribution intentionally reflects that AU academic and DV-sector PA literature is dominant in the published record; recognition-camp voices are concentrated at practising-Bar / specialist-firm level rather than academic level.
